title: The Corner He Grew
caption: Dev brought his grandmother into the community garden with the careful pride of someone carrying good news in both hands. The corner he had tended under Mr. Chen's watchful eye had become more than a patch of soil: tomatoes climbed their ties, marigolds glowed at the edges, and every leaf seemed to show the patience that had gone into it. Chloe watched with her notebook open and felt the sweetness of seeing effort made visible, while Mr. Chen stood nearby with the dry, modest satisfaction of a teacher whose student had quietly surpassed the need for supervision. When Dev's grandmother touched the plants, her face softened with recognition, and she and Mr. Chen fell for a moment into the ease of a shared first language, speaking about patience as if the garden itself had taught it. Dev tried to hand the credit away, bashful as ever, but the moment would not let him hide inside humility. What had grown here was not only food or color, but a steadier version of the boy who had planted it.
location: Willowmere Community Garden, at Dev's flourishing corner in warm early-summer light
story_notes: Dev proudly shows his grandmother the thriving garden corner he has built with Mr. Chen, and she sees how much he has grown alongside it.
